focus: D1 Correctness - guided-run argument and output handling

findingDetails.title: Guided-run parser accepts flag values as the URL or missing flag values as output paths
findingDetails.evidence: parseGuidedRunArgs takes the first non-flag as url and readValue returns args[index+1] without verifying that the value exists or is not another flag.
findingDetails.recommendation: Parse argv with positional/flag consumption, reject missing URL, reject missing --output value, and reject flag-looking values for value-bearing flags.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Compared guided-run parser, documented wrapper contract, and downstream extract parser.

findingDetails.title: Guided-run preflight reports the exact skill root output path as safe
findingDetails.evidence: runPreflight only checks outputPath.startsWith(SKILL_ROOT + path.sep), so outputPath === SKILL_ROOT is not rejected even though extract.ts rejects that exact path later.
findingDetails.recommendation: Mirror extract.ts by rejecting outputPath === SKILL_ROOT as well as descendants, so dry-run/readiness output is truthful.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Compared guided-run preflight against extract.ts output guard.

noveltyJustification: First pass found one required CLI parsing defect and one preflight parity advisory.
ruledOut: extract.ts itself rejects exact skill-root output paths before writing extraction artifacts.

claims.claim: Guided-run can accept missing positional or flag values instead of failing usage before extraction planning.
claims.counterevidenceSought: Checked extract.ts for downstream URL/output validation and guided-run tests for missing-value coverage.
claims.alternativeExplanation: Downstream extract.ts has its own output guard, but guided-run still owns preflight and command planning and does not validate missing URL or missing --output values itself.
claims.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade to P2 if guided-run delegates all argument validation to extract.ts and adds tests proving no output directory or network action can start with malformed guided-run arguments.

focus: D2 Security - trust boundary and write-scope checks
noveltyJustification: Security pass found no new P0/P1; output and digest checks generally fail closed.
ruledOut: No command-surface drift: design-command-surface-check --json reported status pass and drift [].
No numeric-law completeness issue: numeric_law_check reported ok true.
No variant-parameter coverage issue: variant_parameter_check reported ok true.

focus: D3 Traceability - skill contracts, command metadata, and benchmark report parity

findingDetails.title: Human skill-benchmark report omits advisory signals computed in the JSON aggregate
findingDetails.evidence: aggregate records modePrecision, relativeRanking, routeTelemetry, and recipeMissRate, but renderReport's advisory section emits only D4_task_outcome and assetRecall.
findingDetails.recommendation: Render the computed advisory signals in report.md, at least routeTelemetry, recipeMissRate, modePrecision, and relativeRanking summaries.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Compared score-skill-benchmark aggregate fields against build-report markdown renderer.

results.summary: Core sk-design routing claims align; benchmark JSON-to-Markdown parity is partial because several computed advisory signals are not visible in the human report.
The inherited charter's named checks have concrete code/test surfaces and the command-surface run returned pass.
Hub and agents agree on core workflow; runtime permission surfaces drift.
The OpenCode agent grants WebFetch/external-directory capabilities not mirrored by Claude or Codex definitions.

noveltyJustification: Traceability found one new dual-report advisory and confirmed core protocols with one partial report-parity gap.
ruledOut: No extra graph-metadata identity under mode packets: find returned only .opencode/skills/sk-design/graph-metadata.json.

focus: D4 Maintainability - runtime parity and long-term operability

findingDetails.title: OpenCode design agent has broader permissions than Claude and Codex design agents
findingDetails.evidence: OpenCode grants webfetch and external_directory while the Claude agent lists Read/Write/Edit/Bash/Grep/Glob plus memory and Codex uses workspace-write without WebFetch parity.
findingDetails.recommendation: Either document this as an intentional runtime-specific capability or align the design agent permission surfaces so cross-runtime behavior is predictable.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Compared all three runtime design agent definitions.

noveltyJustification: Maintainability pass found one advisory runtime-parity drift; no P0/P1 beyond the parser finding.
ruledOut: The core workflow text is materially equivalent across all three agent files.

focus: Stabilization - replay active findings and stop gates

results.summary: Partial remains due human report advisory omission.
Named deterministic gates had source evidence and clean observed output where run.

noveltyJustification: Stabilization pass introduced no new findings and replayed active evidence.
ruledOut: No P0 discovered after re-reading F001/F002/F003/F004 evidence.
